    Home › Aircraft › Airbus A320 family

    Airbus A320 family

    Airbus · Narrowbody · In production

    The world's best-selling airliner family.

    Length44.51 m
    Wingspan35.8 m
    Range7,400 km
    MTOW97 t
    Typical seats180
    Built11,800+
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#19 of 30
    4.04 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#4 of 40
    76.3 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#37 of 43
    0.80 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#36 of 43
    2.18 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#23 of 40
    8.5 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Weight per seat#23 of 30
    539 kg MTOW ÷ typical seats
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Airbus A320 family's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Airbus A320 family35.8 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #37 of 43 by length → #36 by weight → #30 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length (A321neo)44.51 m
    Wingspan35.80 m
    Height11.76 m
    MTOW (A321XLR)97,000 kg
    Range (A321XLR)8,700 km
    Typical seats (A321neo)180–220
    Max speed871 km/h (Mach 0.82)
    Engines2 × CFM LEAP-1A or PW1100G
    First flight (A320)22 February 1987
    Delivered (family)11,800+

    Compare it

    Put the Airbus A320 next to something

    vs Embraer E-Jet E2 →vs Boeing 737 →
    vs Boeing 737 vs Airbus A220 Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousAirbus A300 Next →Airbus A330

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The A320 did something no airliner had done before: it handed the controls to a computer. Fly-by-wire with flight-envelope protection, sidesticks instead of yokes, and a glass cockpit — in 1987, when the competition still flew cables and pulleys. Every Airbus since has been built on that architecture, and pilots can cross-qualify across the whole family because of it.

    It also won the numbers war. The A320 family overtook the 737 in cumulative orders, and the A321neo — 44.51 metres long, transatlantic-capable in XLR form — has become the aircraft that reshaped medium-haul flying, opening routes a narrowbody had no business flying.

    Almost 12,000 have been delivered across the family, and the backlog runs years deep. If you have flown in the last decade, the odds are very good it was on one of these.

    Variants

    The Airbus A320 family line

    A318 / A319
    The short ones — the A318 is nicknamed the Baby Bus.
    A320
    The original, and the family's heart.
    A321 / A321neo
    The 44.51 m stretch. Spec shown above.
    A321XLR
    The transatlantic narrowbody — 8,700 km from a single aisle.
    Operators

    Who flies the Airbus A320 family

    The top 10 operators by fleet size, of ≈350 worldwide — the rest are aggregated below. Counts marked ≈ are approximate, verified 2026-07-14.

    AAAmerican Airlines≈480→
    INIndiGoThe world's fastest-growing A320 operator.≈380
    MUChina Eastern Airlines≈350→
    GBeasyJet≈340
    UAUnited Airlines≈330→ CZChina Southern Airlines≈320→ DLDelta Air Lines≈250→
    HUWizz Air≈220
    LHLufthansa≈180→ CAAir China≈180→

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
